policy: A write/destructive capability can be t1-ready only with a recorded active evidence entry or a named legacy exception. Guarded writes must remain fail-closed until live mutation-body evidence is recorded.

activeWrites.bodyShape: transactions[] with block set operations; properties.title uses Notion title array
activeWrites.authShape: same-origin cookie; x-notion-active-user-header and x-notion-space-id only inside bound spec
activeWrites.redaction: no cookie, token, user id, or space id recorded

activeWrites.bodyShape: transactions[] with set operations for title, icon, and cover plus update for object metadata
activeWrites.authShape: same-origin cookie; x-notion-active-user-header and x-notion-space-id only inside bound spec
activeWrites.redaction: no cookie, token, user id, or space id recorded

activeWrites.bodyShape: transactions[] creates collection, collection_view_page, and collection_view records
activeWrites.authShape: same-origin cookie; x-notion-active-user-header and x-notion-space-id only inside bound spec
activeWrites.redaction: no cookie, token, user id, or space id recorded

activeWrites.bodyShape: transactions[] creates a block row and maps property names through collection schema ids
activeWrites.authShape: same-origin cookie; x-notion-active-user-header and x-notion-space-id only inside bound spec
activeWrites.redaction: no cookie, token, user id, or space id recorded

activeWrites.bodyShape: form body contains token carrier, channel, text, and optional thread_ts
activeWrites.authShape: xoxc-like token carrier in body; xoxd-like HttpOnly cookie rides same-origin
activeWrites.verification: legacy active hand-head retained; not precedent for broad slack.send_message activation
activeWrites.redaction: no literal token or cookie value recorded
activeWrites.legacyReason: slack.chat.postMessage predates the Phase 49 activation ledger and has no OpenTabs breadth descriptor; slack.send_message remains guarded fail-closed until fresh live evidence exists
